frameworks.name: evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: perceptual decision via integration of discrete sensory pulses over time

frameworks.name: Poisson pulse statistics
frameworks.one_line: stochastic stimulus design enabling diverse yet controlled evidence streams

core_question: Can head-fixed mice navigating virtual reality accumulate discrete visual pulses of evidence to support perceptual decisions?
key_assumptions: mice can learn virtual T-maze navigation under head fixation
pulse counts on each side provide quantifiable evidence
decision behavior reflects integration rather than single-cue strategies
manipulations: side and number of visual pulses along the T-maze stem
key_findings: many mice across genotypes learn the task at rat-comparable levels
mice are sensitive to single-pulse side differences with stable cue memory
decisions use pulses from throughout the cue region with mild early-cue overweighting
performance depends on evidence magnitude but not duration
interpretations: mice genuinely accumulate sensory evidence rather than relying on single cues
the paradigm is suitable for studying neural mechanisms of evidence integration in mice
